 {"id":"gt-5wb7","title":"Update vision.md with proto/mol/wisp terminology","description":"Update the Steam Engine Metaphor section to use consistent phase terminology:

Current (vision.md):
- Proto molecules = fuel (templates)
- Wisps = steam (transient execution traces)  
- Digests = distillate (permanent records)

New terminology:
- **Proto** = crystal/solid - frozen template
- **Mol** = liquid - reified durable instance (tracked in git)
- **Wisp** = gas - ephemeral instance (evaporates after squash)
- **Digest** = distillate - compressed summary after squash

Key clarification needed:
- Proto → bond → creates either Mol (durable) or Wisp (ephemeral)
- The choice of Mol vs Wisp depends on --wisp flag
- Default is Mol (durable, recorded in main beads)
- Wisp lives in .beads-ephemeral/, squashes away","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:45.68234-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:20:42.826322-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5wb7","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.307488-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-5wtw","title":"Shutdown request handler","description":"Handle polecat shutdown requests:

When polecat runs 'gt handoff --shutdown':
1. Polecat sends mail to <rig>/witness requesting shutdown
2. Witness receives mail
3. Witness verifies:
   - Git working tree is clean
   - Work is submitted (MR exists or in queue)
   - No uncommitted beads changes
4. If clean:
   - gt session stop <rig>/<polecat>
   - git worktree remove polecats/<name>
   - git branch -d polecat/<name>
5. If not clean:
   - Send nudge back to polecat
   - Track retry count

Depends on fixing gt-dsfi (handoff deadlock).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:22.968711-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:28:15.512143-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:28:15.512143-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5wtw","depends_on_id":"gt-53w6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:37.300578-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-5wtw","depends_on_id":"gt-mxyj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:38.893061-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-5xph","title":"Document session cycling protocol in all templates","description":"Add explicit lifecycle request protocol to all agent templates.

## Problem
Templates mention 'request session refresh' but don't show HOW.
Agents don't know the protocol for requesting a cycle.

## Protocol to document
1. Write handoff mail to self (for continuity)
2. Set requesting_cycle=true in state.json
3. Send LIFECYCLE mail to deacon/:
   Subject: 'LIFECYCLE: <role> requesting cycle'
   Body: Reason for cycle request

## Templates to update
- prompts/roles/polecat.md
- prompts/roles/crew.md  
- prompts/roles/witness.md
- prompts/roles/refinery.md

## Also add
- Example state.json location for each role
- When to request cycle (context full, work complete, etc.)
- What happens after (daemon kills, respawns, new session primes)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:54.10282-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:54.10282-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-5y5p","title":"Preflight molecule: verify baseline health before work","description":"Before assigning work, verify baseline (main branch) is healthy.

**From VC**: Self-healing state machine (HEALTHY → SELF_HEALING → ESCALATED). ~200 lines.

**Gas Town implementation**: Preflight molecule or refinery feature:
```yaml
preflight:
  gates: [test, lint, build]
  on_failure: create-fix-issue
```

Run at session start. If baseline broken, file a P0 fix issue and work on that first.

**Value**: Self-healing baseline. Agents don't start from broken state.

**VC lesson**: Prevents cascading failures. Agent shouldn't start work on broken code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:13.807641-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:13.807641-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5y5p","depends_on_id":"gt-zhpa","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:27.469804-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-62hm","title":"Molecule Phase Terminology Documentation","description":"Document the three-phase molecule lifecycle with consistent terminology:

## The Phases (States of Matter)

| Phase | State | Nature |
|-------|-------|--------|
| **Proto** | Solid/Crystal | Frozen template, static definition in catalog |
| **Mol** | Liquid | Reified instance, malleable, tracked in git (durable) |
| **Wisp** | Gas | Evaporates after squash, ephemeral orchestration |

## Documentation Gaps Identified

### High Priority
1. Update vision.md Steam Engine metaphor to use proto/mol/wisp terminology
2. Add lifecycle diagram: Proto → bond → Mol or Wisp → squash → Digest
3. Document Go types for phases (ProtoMolecule, etc.)
4. Document .beads-ephemeral/ structure and purpose

### Medium Priority
5. Update CLAUDE.md files (polecat, witness, refinery) with molecule workflow
6. Document bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI API with examples
7. Add polecat guide: Executing molecules and generating summaries

### Low Priority
8. Add terminology glossary to architecture.md
9. Create troubleshooting playbook for stuck molecules
10. Add reference diagrams for state transitions

## Current State
- architecture.md has good molecule basics but uses inconsistent terminology
- vision.md has Steam Engine metaphor (proto=fuel, wisp=steam, digest=distillate)
- molecules.md has detailed reference but needs phase clarity
- builtin_molecules.go has 8 molecules but no phase documentation in code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:27.537487-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:27.537487-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-7we","title":"Swarms of One: Lightweight single-worker task dispatch","description":"Design and implement a lightweight pattern for firing off single workers to handle tasks without full swarm overhead.

## Context

Currently we have:
- town spawn: Creates a polecat in a rig with issue assignment
- Swarms (sw-*): Full lifecycle tracking with manifest, state, events, reports
- Ephemeral rigs (rig-*): Temporary worker groups for swarms

What's missing: A simple way to say 'fire off a worker to do X' without swarm ceremony.

## Design Questions

1. Should this be a new command like 'gt fire' or an option on existing commands?
2. Should single tasks still get swarm IDs (sw-N) for consistency/queryability?
3. Should it default to creating new workers or support --reuse for idle polecats?
4. How does this relate to crew workers (gt-cik)?

## Possible Interface

gt fire --rig gastown --issue gt-xyz [--prompt '...']
gt fire gastown/QuickTask --issue gt-xyz

## Related

- gt-cik: Overseer Crew (user-managed persistent workspaces)
- gt-kmn: Swarm System epic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:51:02.716629-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-82y","title":"Design: Swarm shutdown and worker cleanup","description":"Design for graceful swarm shutdown, worker cleanup, and session cycling.

## Key Decisions

1. Pre-kill verification uses model intelligence (not framework rules)
2. Witness can request restart when context filling (mail self, exit)
3. Mayor NOT involved in per-worker cleanup (Witness responsibility)
4. Clear responsibility boundaries between Mayor/Witness/Polecat

## Subtasks (implementation)

- gt-sd6: Polecat decommission checklist prompting
- gt-f8v: Witness pre-kill verification protocol
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling and handoff
- gt-gl2: Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities

**Design complete.** Each subtask has full specification in its description.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:44.936374-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:49:22.849598-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T20:12:05.441911-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-83k0","title":"mol-witness-patrol molecule definition","description":"Create mol-witness-patrol in builtin_molecules.go.

## Steps (10 total)
1. inbox-check - Process witness mail (lifecycle, help requests)
2. load-state - Read handoff bead, get nudge counts
3. survey-workers - gt polecat list, categorize by status
4. inspect-workers - tmux capture-pane for each 'working' polecat
5. decide-actions - Apply nudge matrix, queue actions
6. execute-actions - Nudge, kill, or escalate as decided
7. save-state - Update handoff bead with new states
8. generate-summary - Summarize cycle for digest
9. context-check - Check own context usage
10. burn-or-loop - Squash wisp, then loop or cycle session

## Key Behaviors
- Uses wisp storage (.beads-wisp/)
- Reads/writes witness handoff bead for state persistence
- Progressive nudging (3 levels before escalate)
- Pre-kill verification before killing polecats

## Reference
- See prompts/roles/witness.md for protocol details
- See mol-refinery-patrol for similar structure
- Parent epic: gt-aqd8","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:42:43.697249-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T16:42:43.697249-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-8bx","title":"Adaptive backoff for daemon heartbeat","description":"Track agent responsiveness and adjust heartbeat frequency.

## Per-Agent State

```go
type AgentBackoff struct {
    BaseInterval    time.Duration  // 60s default
    CurrentInterval time.Duration  // grows when busy
    MaxInterval     time.Duration  // 10min cap
    ConsecutiveMiss int            // pokes with no response
}
```

## Strategy Options

- **Fixed**: Always 60s (current, simple)
- **Geometric**: 60s → 90s → 135s → 202s (factor 1.5)
- **Exponential**: 60s → 120s → 240s (factor 2, aggressive)

## Recovery

When agent responds (runs a command):
- Reset ConsecutiveMiss to 0
- Return to BaseInterval immediately

## Benefits

- Reduces noise for busy agents
- Saves resources during quiet periods
- Still catches stuck agents (max interval cap)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:33.083844-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:09:31.613912-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:09:31.613912-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8bx","depends_on_id":"gt-bfd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.912289-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-c92","title":"CLI: all command for batch polecat operations","description":"Batch operations across multiple polecats.

## Commands

### gt all start
```
gt all start [--awake-only] [<specs>...]
```
Start sessions for multiple polecats.
- --awake-only: Only start awake polecats
- specs: Polecat names, rig/polecat patterns

### gt all stop
```
gt all stop [<specs>...] [--force]
```
Stop sessions for multiple polecats.

### gt all status
```
gt all status [<specs>...] [--json]
```
Show status of multiple polecats.

### gt all attach
```
gt all attach [<specs>...]
```
Attach to multiple sessions in tmux panes/windows.

### gt all run
```
gt all run <command> [<specs>...]
```
Run command in multiple polecat sessions.

## Spec Patterns
- `Toast`: Specific polecat (in default/current rig)
- `gastown/Toast`: Specific rig/polecat
- `gastown/*`: All polecats in rig
- `*`: All polecats everywhere

## Implementation
```go
func expandSpecs(specs []string, awakeOnly bool) ([]*polecat.Polecat, error) {
    // Expand patterns to list of polecats
}

func runForAll(polecats []*polecat.Polecat, action func(*polecat.Polecat) error) error {
    // Run action for each, collect errors
}
```

## New File
internal/cmd/all.go

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/all_cmd.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pattern expansion works
- [ ] Parallel execution where safe
- [ ] Aggregate error reporting
- [ ] --awake-only filter works","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:12.411789-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:47.255503-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-caih","title":"Witness handoff bead state persistence","description":"Implement state persistence for Witness across wisp cycles.

## Problem
Wisps burn between cycles, but Witness needs to remember:
- Which workers have been nudged
- How many times (nudge_count)
- When was last nudge
- Last observed activity

## Solution
Witness handoff bead with worker_states field:

```json
{
  "id": "gt-witness-state",
  "type": "handoff",
  "assignee": "<rig>/witness",
  "pinned": true,
  "worker_states": {
    "furiosa": {
      "issue": "gt-123",
      "nudge_count": 2,
      "last_nudge": "2024-12-22T10:00:00Z"
    }
  },
  "last_patrol": "2024-12-22T10:05:00Z"
}
```

## Implementation
1. On patrol start: bd show <witness-handoff-id> to load state
2. During patrol: update in-memory state
3. On save-state step: bd update to persist
4. State survives wisp burn/squash

 {"id":"gt-caz","title":"Timed Beads: Scheduled recurring work","description":"## Summary

Timed beads wake up periodically and get injected into the ready queue by the daemon.

## Schema Extension

```yaml
id: gt-weekly-sync
type: task  # or sentinel
schedule: "0 9 * * 1"  # cron: Monday 9am
# OR
interval: 24h          # every 24 hours
tier: haiku            # cheap model for routine checks
next_run: 2025-12-20T09:00:00Z
```

## Daemon Integration

Daemon heartbeat loop:
1. Check timed beads where `next_run <= now`
2. For each due bead:
   - Inject into ready queue (set status to open if needed)
   - Update `next_run` based on schedule/interval
3. Witnesses pick up work via `bd ready`

## Use Cases

- Weekly team sync reminders
- Daily health checks
- Periodic cleanup tasks
- Scheduled reports

## Interaction with Pinned Beads

A pinned bead can be timed - it wakes up periodically but never closes.
This is how you model "background services" in Gas Town.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:39.665294-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:39.665294-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-cik","title":"Overseer Crew: User-managed persistent workspaces","description":"## Overview

Crew workers are the overseer's (human's) personal workspaces within a rig. Unlike polecats which are witness-managed and ephemeral, crew workers are:

- **Persistent**: Not auto-garbage-collected
- **User-managed**: Overseer controls lifecycle
- **Long-lived identities**: dave, emma, fred - recognizable names
- **Gas Town integrated**: Mail, handoff mechanics work
- **Tmux optional**: Can work in terminal directly

## Directory Structure

```
<rig>/
  polecats/       # Managed workers (witness controls)
  refinery/       # Merge queue processor
  witness/        # Pit boss
  crew/           # Overseer's personal workspaces
    dave/         # Full clone, persistent
    emma/         # Full clone, persistent
    fred/         # Full clone, persistent
```

## Key Differences from Polecats

- Location: crew/ instead of polecats/
- Lifecycle: User-managed, not witness-managed
- Auto-cleanup: Never (polecats auto-cleanup on swarm land)
- Issue assignment: Optional (polecats require it)
- Tmux: Optional (polecats require it)
- Mail & Handoff: Yes for both
- Identity: Persistent (polecats are ephemeral)

## CLI Commands

- gt crew add <name> [--rig <rig>] - Create crew workspace
- gt crew list [--rig <rig>] - List crew workspaces
- gt crew at <rig>/<name> - Attach to workspace (start session)
- gt crew attach <name> - Attach (infer rig from cwd)
- gt crew refresh <name> - Handoff + restart (context cycling)
- gt crew remove <name> [--force] - Remove workspace
- gt crew status [<name>] - Show workspace status

## Design Notes

- Crew workers use full git clones (not worktrees)
- Optional beads integration via BEADS_DIR
- Mail-to-self handoff works for context cycling
- No witness monitoring or nudging
- No automatic issue assignment required

## Background

Users often maintain separate repo clones for serial agent work. This is tedious to set up manually. Crew workspaces bring these into Gas Town's infrastructure while keeping user control.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:47:37.529887-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T20:59:46.13518-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T20:59:46.13518-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-j5tk","title":"Work assignment messages should auto-close on completion","description":"When a polecat completes work on an issue, the work assignment message (msg-type:task) stays open. Found 7 stale work assignments in gastown after swarm completed.

Proposal: When bd close is called on an issue, auto-close any work assignment messages that reference that issue in their body.

Alternative: Work assignment messages could use a different lifecycle - perhaps they should be acked (closed) when the polecat starts working, not when they finish.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:12:28.403974-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:12:28.403974-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-j6s8","title":"Refinery startup: bond mol-refinery-patrol on start","description":"Wire up Refinery to automatically bond its patrol molecule on startup.

## Current state
- mol-refinery-patrol exists in builtin_molecules.go
- prompts/roles/refinery.md describes the protocol
- Refinery doesn't auto-bond on startup

## Desired behavior
On Refinery session start:
1. gt prime detects RoleRefinery
2. Check for existing in-progress patrol: bd list --status=in_progress --assignee=refinery
3. If found: resume from current step
4. If not found: bd mol bond mol-refinery-patrol --wisp
5. Output patrol context to agent

## Implementation options
A) Add to gt prime (outputRefineryPatrolContext)
B) Add startup hook in refinery CLAUDE.md
C) Both (prime detects, template reinforces)

## Testing
- Start refinery session
- Verify patrol bonds automatically
- Kill mid-patrol, restart, verify resumes

## Depends on

 {"id":"gt-j87","title":"Design: Work flow simulation and validation","description":"Validate GGT designs through simulation before implementation.

## Validation Approaches

### 1. Dry-Run Simulation (Recommended First)
Mayor walks through scenarios mentally/on paper:
- "If polecat Toast signals done with dirty git state, what happens?"
- "If Witness context fills mid-verification, what state is lost?"
- "If two polecats try to close same issue, what happens?"

Create beads for any gaps discovered.

### 2. Real Work in gastown-py
Use Python Gas Town to stress-test assumptions:
- Run actual batch work on test repos
- Observe edge cases in practice
- Document issues found

### 3. Edge Case Analysis
Systematic review of failure modes:
- Agent crashes mid-operation
- Network failures during sync
- Concurrent access to shared state
- Context limits hit at bad times

## Key Scenarios to Validate

- [ ] Witness session cycling (state preservation)
- [ ] Polecat decommission with dirty state
- [ ] Merge conflicts in queue
- [ ] Beads sync conflicts between workers
- [ ] Escalation path (stuck worker -> Mayor)
- [ ] Cross-rig communication
- [ ] Federation mail routing (future)

## Success Criteria

- No data loss scenarios identified
- Clear recovery paths for all failure modes
- Edge cases either handled or documented as limitations
- Design improves as model cognition improves

## Output

For each scenario validated:
1. Document in relevant bead if issue found
2. Create new beads for missing functionality
3. Update architecture.md if design changes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:24:11.251841-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:25:49.858717-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-jpt","title":"Town-level beads: Real DB for coordination mail","description":"Implement Option A from mail redesign: Town gets real beads DB for coordination.

## Background

Mail is now Beads. But currently:
- Town .beads/redirect points to rig beads
- mayor/mail/ has legacy JSONL files
- Cross-rig coordination has no clear home

## Design

Town beads = coordination, cross-rig mail, mayor inbox, handoffs
Rig beads = project issues, work items

Matches HOP hierarchy: platform > project > worker

## Structure

~/gt/
  .beads/           # REAL beads DB (prefix: gm-)
  mayor/
    town.json
    state.json      # NO mail/ directory
  gastown/
    .beads/         # Rig beads (prefix: ga-)

## Tasks

1. Delete ~/gt/.beads/redirect
2. Run bd init --prefix gm at ~/gt/ (town beads)
3. Delete ~/gt/mayor/mail/ directory
4. Update gt mail to use beads not JSONL
5. Add mail fields (thread_id, reply_to, msg_type)
6. Update gt prime for two-tier model
7. Update docs/architecture.md

## Addressing

- mayor/ -> town beads
- rig/agent -> rig beads
- Cross-rig -> town beads","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T19:09:55.855955-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.032558-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.032558-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-lek6","title":"gt rig reset --stale: Clear orphaned in_progress items","description":"Reset in_progress issues when their assigned agent no longer exists.

## Problem
When polecats die without cleanup, their issues remain in_progress forever.
Need a way to bulk-reset these orphaned items.

## Command
```bash
gt rig reset --stale [--dry-run]
```

## Logic
For each in_progress issue in rig:
1. Parse assignee (e.g., "gastown/furiosa")
2. Map to tmux session name (gt-gastown-furiosa)
3. If session does NOT exist:
   - Reset status to "open"
   - Clear assignee
4. Exception: skip crew/* assignees (persistent identities)
   OR check if crew tmux session exists

## Output
```
Resetting stale work in gastown:
  gt-abc: gastown/furiosa (no session) → open
  gt-def: gastown/nux (no session) → open
  Skipped: gt-xyz: gastown/crew/max (persistent)
Reset 2 issues, skipped 1
```

## Related
- gt-2kz: CLI cleanup commands for stale state
- gt-rdmw: orphan-check in deacon patrol
- gt-orphans command (list orphaned molecules)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:33:46.962413-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:16:22.295127-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:16:22.295127-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented --stale flag for gt rig reset with dry-run support"}

 {"id":"gt-lno","title":"Swarm state persistence: manifest + state + events","description":"Upgrade swarm persistence to match PGT pattern.

## Current State
Single .gastown/swarms.json with all swarms.

## Target State (per-swarm)
```
.gastown/swarms/
└── <swarm-id>/
    ├── manifest.json  # Immutable config
    ├── state.json     # Mutable progress
    ├── events.jsonl   # Audit log
    └── report.md      # Generated report
```

## File Schemas

### manifest.json (immutable after creation)
```json
{
  "id": "sw-1",
  "title": "Epic description",
  "epic_id": "gt-abc",
  "rig": "gastown",
  "base_commit": "abc123",
  "integration_branch": "swarm/sw-1",
  "target_branch": "main",
  "workers": ["Toast", "Nux"],
  "tasks": [{"id": "gt-xyz", "title": "..."}],
  "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}
```

### state.json (mutable)
```json
{
  "state": "active",
  "task_states": {
    "gt-xyz": {"state": "merged", "assignee": "Toast"}
  },
  "updated_at": "2024-01-01T01:00:00Z",
  "error": null
}
```

### events.jsonl (append-only audit)
```jsonl
{"event": "created", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_assigned", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_merged", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
```

## Benefits
- Audit trail via events.jsonl
- Manifest immutability prevents corruption
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Per-swarm isolation

## Migration
Keep backward compat with swarms.json during transition.

## Files to Modify
- internal/swarm/manager.go: Refactor persistence
- internal/cmd/swarm.go: SwarmStore → directory-based

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-swarm directory structure
- [ ] Events logged to JSONL
- [ ] Manifest immutable after creation
- [ ] List command aggregates from directories","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:14.151538-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-qsvq","title":"gt doctor: Detect and clean up orphaned sessions/processes","description":"## Problem
Orphaned tmux sessions and Claude processes accumulate over time, consuming memory.

## Discovered Orphans
- gt-deacon: Idle Claude session, not part of any rig

## Proposed Solution

### gt doctor --check (default)
Detect issues without fixing:
- Orphan sessions (not matching rig/polecat/crew/witness/refinery/mayor pattern)
- Claude processes without parent tmux session
- Tmux sessions without Claude (stuck at bash prompt)
- Polecats marked 'working' but session idle

### gt doctor --fix
Clean up detected issues:
- Kill orphan sessions
- Kill orphan Claude processes
- Optionally reset stuck polecat state

### gt gc (alternative name)
Short alias for cleanup operations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects orphan sessions
- [ ] Detects orphan processes
- [ ] Safe cleanup (doesn't kill active work)
- [ ] Reports what was cleaned","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T01:06:39.046959-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T07:54:51.210265-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T07:54:51.210265-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-rm3","title":"CLI: gt refinery commands (start, stop, status, queue)","description":"CLI commands for managing the Refinery agent.

## Commands

```bash
gt refinery start <rig>      # Start refinery for a rig
gt refinery stop <rig>       # Stop refinery
gt refinery status <rig>     # Show refinery status
gt refinery queue <rig>      # Show merge queue
```

## gt refinery start

Starts the Refinery daemon for the specified rig.

Options:
- --foreground: Run in foreground (default: background)
- --auto-merge: Enable auto-merge (default: from config)

## gt refinery stop

Stops a running Refinery. Gracefully finishes current MR if processing.

## gt refinery status

Shows:
- Running state (running/stopped)
- Current MR being processed (if any)
- Queue length
- Last merge time
- Recent activity

## gt refinery queue

Shows the merge queue:
```
Merge queue for 'wyvern':
  1. [pending] Toast/polecat-auth-fix (15m ago)
  2. [pending] Capable/polecat-new-feature (5m ago)
  
1 merged today, 0 rejected
```

## Implementation

Uses gt-ov2 (Refinery agent) for daemon functionality.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:24.754361-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rm3","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:30.679909-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

Polecats with attached work should also auto-continue.

## Current behavior
outputCrewAttachmentStatus() in prime.go:
- Only runs for RoleCrew
- Outputs '→ AUTO-CONTINUE MODE' when attached work detected

## Desired behavior
- Rename to outputAttachmentStatus() or similar
- Run for RoleCrew AND RolePolecat
- Same directive: if attachment exists, work immediately

## The Propulsion Principle
'If you find something on your hook, YOU RUN IT.'

This applies to ALL workers, not just crew.

## Implementation
1. Extend role check in outputCrewAttachmentStatus()
2. Adjust assignee detection for polecats vs crew
3. Test with both worker types","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:22.149252-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:22.149252-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-rr1i","title":"mol-swarm-cleanup: Post-swarm debris cleanup molecule","description":"After a 20+ worker swarm completed, found significant beads debris:
- 18 stale messages (work assignments, lifecycle requests, swarm instructions)

- Test messages accumulated

Need: Document a post-swarm checklist or create gt swarm cleanup command that:
1. Closes stale work assignment messages

+{"id":"gt-rt6g","title":"Deacon: Don't heartbeat Mayor during active work or human input","description":"Update deacon prompting to be smarter about when to heartbeat the Mayor:

1. Don't heartbeat if the Mayor appears to be actively working (recent activity)
2. Don't heartbeat if the Mayor is waiting for human input (e.g., asked a question)
3. Only heartbeat if the Mayor seems to have been idle for a long time

The deacon should check for signs of activity before nudging, to avoid interrupting productive work or blocking on legitimate user input.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:00:28.667896-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:00:36.803457-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-s3m0","title":"gt polecat: add 'done' or 'finish' command to transition from working to idle","description":"When a polecat finishes work but session wasn't properly cleaned up, there's no way to reset it from 'working' state back to 'idle'.

Tried:
```
gt polecat sleep gastown/Angharad
Error: sleeping polecat: polecat is not active (state: working)
```

The sleep command only works on 'active' polecats, not 'working' ones.

Had to manually edit state.json to reset:
```
jq '.state = "idle" | .issue = ""' state.json
```

Need a command like:
```
gt polecat done gastown/Angharad   # working -> idle
gt polecat finish gastown/Angharad # working -> idle  
gt polecat reset gastown/Angharad  # any state -> idle (force)
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T01:41:39.851037-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.031611-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.031611-08:00"}
